name: Fig Newtons # 2
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, beat butter and sour cream until light.
2. Gradually add brown sugar, beating until mixture is very light & thick.
3. Add eggs one at a time; beat in vanilla. Sift together flour, baking powder, soda, cinnamon, and salt. Add to creamed mixture, beating on low speed or by hand. Mix well and turn out onto a sheet of floured plastic wrap; cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours.
4. Cut heavy stems off figs. Combine figs and water in a saucepan and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes, or until fruit is soft.
5. Add sugar, lemon rind, and salt and simmer, covered, for an additional 15 minutes. Press mixture through a food mill and cool.
6. Preheat oven to 350°F. Cover a baking sheet with parchment paper or spray with cooking spray.
7. Divide the chilled dough into thirds and refrigerate one part. Roll the remaining piece on a well floured surface until it forms a rectangle about 5 x 11 inches.
8. Spread one third of fig filling slightly to one side of the center, along the length of the dough, leaving a 1/2 inch margin around the edges. Mound the filling slightly in the center along the whole length. Moisten the edges with water.
9. Gently lift the uncovered pastry and fold over the filling. Press the two halves together to seal. Trim the edges and shape the roll into a neat, half-cylinder form about 11 inches long and 2-2 1/2 inches wide.
10. Place the roll on the prepared baking sheet. Repeat with the remaining dough and filling. Bake for 25 minutes, or until slightly browned. Cool rolls somewhat on a rack, then trim off the ends and, with a sharp serrated knife, cut each one into slices about 1 1/2 inches wide. Replace slices on the rack. When bars are completely cool, store them airtight.
